title: Caramelized Japanese Chicken Ramen
description: A comforting, creamy ramen that caramelizes tender chicken in a savory stock, tailored as a hangover cure and FODMAP-friendly.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of sesame oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chicken thighs and sear until browned on both sides, about 4 minutes; remove and set aside.
In the same pot, add the remaining tablespoon of sesame oil and grated ginger, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the carob powder and miso paste, then pour in the chicken stock and water; bring to a gentle simmer.
Return the chicken to the pot, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the chicken is tender and the stock thickens slightly.
Stir in the grated carrot and cucumber ribbons, cooking for 3 minutes until softened.
Meanwhile, cook the ramen noodles in a separate pot according to package instructions, draining well.
Divide the noodles into bowls, ladle the hot chicken and sauce over them, and garnish with scallion greens and toasted sesame seeds before serving.
